title: World War I
description: This exercise will cover the years leading up to as well as the cause, course, and consequences of World War I through the themes of GOV and TEC. Items in this exercise will model changing global power, causes and consequences of World War 1, and methods of war. Each model will scale 3 items, for a total of 12.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following general themes best characterize common challenges to the existing social and political order from 1900 to the present?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: intensifying resistance against capitalism and empires
the rise of movements against gender and racial equality
the decline of  multinational corporations
the fragmentation of traditional international institutions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best summarizes continuities in Mexican society throughout the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: gender inequality and class divisions
term limits in government and the power of oil companies
the influence of the United States and the lack of labor unions
limited legal rights and the unequal distribution of land

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true for both the Mexican and the Russian Revolutions?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Economic and environmental conditions deteriorated in the years leading up to the revolution.
Violent revolutions led to minimal social change in the long term.
Cultural movements of artists led to revolutionary action.
Communities were relatively homogenous, leading to united revolutionary fronts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following internal factors most contributed to the success of the Russian Revolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The development of a class identity unified workers from socially and geographically diverse areas.
A rising sense of nationalism created fervor for political change.
A strong sense of Russian culture linked people from socially and geographically diverse areas.
The outbreak of World War I led to many Russian deaths and shortages of food and fuel.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did technological change impact governance in the years leading up to World War I?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Industrial technology promoted militarism, while lagging communication technology hindered diplomacy.
New technology made the world seem closer together, creating new conflicts between people far away.
Military technology was concentrated in a single area of the world, influencing international relations.
Governments used technological advancements brought on by industrialization to avoid war.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following most directly summarizes the causes of World War I?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: imperialist expansion, a flawed alliance system, industrial militarism, and intense nationalism
assassination attempts, nationalization of resources, and the rise of communism
industrialization, the organization of labor, and global rebellions against colonial powers
economic hardships in Europe, industrial militarism, the rise of fascism, and appeasement

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following examples best represents the broad economic impacts of World War I?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cost of war led to an increase in the extraction of raw materials to recoup lost profits.
Larger countries emerged from the war as economic leaders and lent money to smaller countries.
Since many countries struggled to fund the war, income taxes became commonplace in the 1920s.
World War I universally destroyed the global economy, causing hardship for colonies and colonial powers alike.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains how industrialization was both a cause and an effect of World War I?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It fueled imperialism and helped to recover the cost of war.
It created colonies and led to a more militarized world.
It fostered nationalism and led to the emergence of communism.
It increased communication technology and helped to further link countries together after the war.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the most accurate example of the global nature of World War I?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Entente forces took over most German colonies in Africa by 1915.
Mexico joined the war with Germany, bringing the Americas into the conflict.
Manufactured goods shipped to Europe from their colonies fueled the war.
Chinese shipbuilders provided Germany with new submarine technology at the start of the war.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which aspect of World War I does the above image best illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Drawing of three women working on a machine with the title, “Women are working day and night to win the war”.
options.caption: War poster, Witherby & Co. London, 1915. Public domain.

choices.content: total war
gender equality
use of chemical weapons
imperialism

assessment_items.question_content:
**Trench warfare and the industrial development of weaponry, such as machine guns and poisonous gas, contributed to which of the following social changes in Europe in the 1920s?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: disillusionment
gender equality
class consciousness
economic hardship

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>Bent double, like old beggars under sacks,
>
>Knock-kneed, coughing like hags, we cursed through sludge,
>
>Till on the haunting flares we turned our backs,
>
>And towards our distant rest began to trudge.
>
>Men marched asleep. Many had lost their boots,
>
>But limped on, blood-shod. All went lame; all blind;
>
>Drunk with fatigue; deaf even to the hoots
>
>Of gas-shells dropping softly behind.Gas! GAS! Quick, boys!—An ecstasy of fumbling
>
>Fitting the clumsy helmets just in time,
>
>But someone still was yelling out and stumbling
>
>And flound’ring like a man in fire or lime

Source: Wilfred Edward Salter Owen, “Dulce et decorum est”, poem written October 1917, Craiglockhart War Hospital, United Kingdom. 

**War technologies like what is described above most contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the lost generation
the development of machine guns
economic hardship
the Geneva Conventions
